score_reason: Response echoes the full prompt verbatim and appends only a contextual parenthetical note. Zero analysis of failure modes mechanisms amendments or trade-offs. Degenerate output.

Correctly identifies the replica-lag race condition as the double-charge mechanism. Plausible explanation for why testing missed it. Two architectural fixes named with basic trade-off mention. Monitoring suggestion is generic but directionally correct. Minor issues: fix descriptions are shallow and trade-offs lack depth.

Produces a board statement that acknowledges problems and mentions LOIs. However milestones are contradictory (LOIs already signed yet milestone says secure them). Indirect handling of CPO position is crude and partially defensive. Tone misses the nuance requested. Major structural issues prevent a passing score.

Recommendation restates each team position separately rather than synthesizing a unified concrete recommendation. No resolution of the 2-week vs 3-month conflict. No cost constraint addressed. Does not fulfill the 3-4 sentence unified recommendation requirement.

Hallucinates four extra employees not present in the input (Eve Frank George Harry). Produces no YAML transformation — output is the extended flat list followed by hundreds of blank lines and whitespace characters. Degenerate output.

Schedule is invalid on multiple constraints: places Alice-Dave 1:1 at 10am when Alice is unavailable 10-11am; duplicates Team sync twice (9am and 3pm) creating 5 meetings instead of 3; introduces a phantom Dave-Alice meeting. Explanation of validity is incorrect.

Correctly identifies both key inconsistencies: body format (JSON vs form-encoded) and rate limit (100/min vs 1000/hr). Minor issues: uses LaTeX markup unnecessarily; misses noting that 100 req/min and 100 req/min are consistent between A and B on rate while only C diverges. Core findings are accurate.

JSON output is fully correct with all four required fields (name role department start_date) accurately extracted. Minor issue: appends an unnecessary Note about date format and echoes the prompt instructions before the JSON instead of outputting only JSON as instructed.

Hallucinates extra list items not in the input. Output format is wrong — shows item-frequency pairs instead of a deduplicated sorted numbered list. Correct output would be 1.Apple 2.banana 3.cherry 4.Date with count 4. Response is fundamentally incorrect on all steps.
Response is the prompt echoed back with 1 completion token. No comparison written. Effectively empty output.
